rationale.R1.1: Load-bearing: 'reframe the conceptual story' is a direct instruction to reconstruct the thesis, gap statement, and contribution hierarchy. The artifact currently fractures across three distinct claim clusters — the CPO method (empirical), the O(√T) regret bound (theoretical, labelled 'principal contribution'), and the benchmark suite (infrastructural) — and assigns primacy to the theory. Deciding whether that assignment is correct, and reorganising the narrative around an alternative centre of gravity, is the entire task.
rationale.R1.4: Central: selecting the correct reframe requires diagnosing the actual novelty delta of each claim cluster against prior work. The regret bound, the ranking method, and the benchmark suite each carry different novelty profiles; the strongest reframe follows the strongest delta, so novelty assessment is a prerequisite for any defensible reconceptualisation.
